task_completion.description: Open Blender and locate the target object
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender is open. 'BaseCube' is visible in the Outliner panel or selected in the 3D Viewport.

task_completion.description: Set up Image Texture nodes for baking
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Shader Editor is visible, showing new Image Texture nodes created for the BaseCube's material. These nodes act as the target for the baked textures and should be set to 1024x1024 resolution.
In the Render Properties panel, the Render Engine is set to 'Cycles' and the Bake Type is set to 'Ambient Occlusion'.
In the Render Properties panel, the Bake Type is set to 'Diffuse'. Under the Influence/Contributions settings, only 'Color' is selected (Direct and Indirect lighting must be unchecked).

task_completion.description: Save the baked AO and Diffuse textures
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Image Editor or a File Browser dialog shows the textures being saved as 'baked_ao.png' and 'baked_diffuse.png' in the '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' directory.
A File Save dialog or the Blender window title bar indicates the project is being saved as 'bake_project.blend' in the '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Blender GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through Blender's menus, Shader Editor, and Render Properties, rather than just running a pre-written Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Agent actually baked the textures in Blender
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Texture Bake' progress bar at the bottom of the Blender interface, or the Image Editor displaying the unwrapped UV layout of the baked textures (a predominantly red image for diffuse, and a grayscale image with darker edges for AO).

integrity.description: Agent did not fake the output images using external tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not open an external image editor (like GIMP) to paint the textures, nor did it use terminal commands to download or copy pre-existing images into the target directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target object is 'BaseCube'. The expected outputs are two 1024x1024 PNG images: an Ambient Occlusion map saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/baked_ao.png' and a Diffuse Color map saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/baked_diffuse.png'. The Blender project must be saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/bake_project.blend'. The Diffuse map should be predominantly red (the color of the cube) and the AO map should show geometric variation (grayscale with darker edges due to beveling).
